articles.title: NCAA Hoops Are Biggest Kalshi Market as March Madness Nears
articles.summary: NCAA basketball betting has become Kalshi's largest market as March Madness approaches, drawing massive trading volume from sports bettors looking for regulated alternatives to traditional sportsbooks. The surge signals prediction markets are successfully capturing mainstream sports betting demand, potentially reshaping how Americans wager on major sporting events.

articles.title: Free Press Opinion: Prediction markets shouldn’t be so easy to play
articles.summary: The Free Press argues that prediction markets have become too accessible and user-friendly for casual participants. If regulators buy this argument, we could see new barriers that price out retail bettors and limit the liquidity that makes these markets actually predictive.

articles.title: Exclusive | Kalshi and Polymarket Are Each Eyeing Roughly $20 Billion Valuations
articles.summary: Both Kalshi and Polymarket are reportedly seeking valuations around $20 billion as they pursue major funding rounds or potential public offerings. These astronomical valuations signal that prediction markets have officially crossed from niche trading curiosity to Wall Street's next big obsession, which means serious money and regulatory attention are about to flood the space.

articles.title: Popular prediction markets take heat from lawmakers
articles.summary: Congress is targeting prediction markets with new regulatory scrutiny, likely sparked by their explosive growth during election season. The crackdown could force major platforms to restrict US users or face hefty fines, potentially killing the momentum that's made political betting mainstream.

articles.title: Are Prediction Markets A Threat To Horse Racing?
articles.summary: Horse racing traditionalists are reportedly worried that prediction markets could steal betting volume from their centuries-old industry. If political betting becomes mainstream, it could normalize prediction markets enough to make horse racing's parimutuel system look antiquated by comparison.

articles.title: Prediction markets are luring teenage gamblers in 2026
articles.summary: Prediction markets are attracting underage users who treat political and economic forecasting like sports betting, raising serious regulatory red flags. Expect stricter age verification requirements and potential crackdowns that could force platforms to choose between compliance costs and market access.

articles.title: Hell Gate is Leaning in to A.I. and Prediction Markets, What Could Go Wrong?
articles.summary: Hell Gate, the NYC news site known for its snarky local coverage, is apparently diving headfirst into AI and prediction markets despite the obvious potential for chaos. When a publication that thrives on exposing municipal incompetence starts playing with betting markets, smart money says they'll either create genuinely useful political prediction content or spectacularly flame out in ways that make great case studies.

articles.title: Hot Copy: Prediction markets still dominating headlines
articles.summary: Prediction markets continue grabbing mainstream media attention across major outlets, cementing their role as the go-to source for election forecasting and political analysis. The sustained coverage signals prediction markets have officially crossed into permanent fixture territory, which means more liquidity, sharper odds, and better opportunities for informed bettors.

articles.title: $100K Polymarket Winner Wagers on US Forces Entering Iran
articles.summary: A Polymarket trader who recently netted $100K is now betting that US military forces will enter Iranian territory. This signals either insider confidence in escalating Middle East tensions or a high-stakes gamble that could move markets if other whales follow the lead.

articles.title: New Prediction Markets Insider Trading Bill Surfaces In Congress
articles.summary: A new congressional bill targets insider trading in prediction markets, apparently aiming to regulate how non-public information gets used in political and event betting. This could fundamentally reshape how prediction markets operate by creating clearer legal boundaries around what information traders can legally act on.

articles.title: The prediction market backlash has arrived
articles.summary: Critics are finally pushing back against the prediction market hype, questioning their accuracy and influence on everything from elections to policy decisions. The backlash threatens to slow mainstream adoption just as platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi were gaining serious institutional credibility and regulatory wins.

articles.title: 2 young billionaires are behind the prediction market boom. They hate each other
articles.summary: The CEOs of America's two biggest prediction market platforms are publicly feuding, turning what should be a growing industry into a personal grudge match. This executive drama matters because when the heads of Kalshi and Polymarket are busy throwing punches instead of building better products, it gives traditional betting companies and regulators more ammunition to dismiss prediction markets as immature toys rather than serious financial instruments.
